zh.lifeSummary: 當Jesse R. Smith Sr.於1703年1月31日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦時，他的父親Thomas Smith Sr.55歲，母親Mary Mayo42歲。他於1724年7月4日與Sarah Higgins在美國麻薩諸塞州巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1782年6月14日在美國紐約州普特南卡梅爾享年79歲，埋葬在美國紐約州度斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jesse R. Smith Sr.于1703年1月31日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦时，他的父亲Thomas Smith Sr.55岁，母亲Mary Mayo42岁。他于1724年7月4日与Sarah Higgins在美国马萨诸塞州巴恩斯塔波易亞斯坦结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1782年6月14日在美国纽约州普特南卡梅爾享年79岁，埋葬在美国纽约州度斯。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Jesse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Meaning ‘gift’ in Hebrew; it is borne by the father of King David (1 Samuel 16), from whose line (according to the New Testament) Jesus was ultimately descended. It was popular among the Puritans, and is still used frequently in the United States, less so in Britain. As a girl's name it is a respelling of   Jessie .  Notable American bearers have included the outlaw  Jesse James  (  1847–82 ), the athlete  Jesse Owens  (  1913–80 ), and the politician  Jesse Jackson  (  b. 1941 ).

variants.name: Jessica
meanings: Apparently of Shakespearean origin. This was the name of the daughter of Shylock in The Merchant of Venice ( 1596 ). Shakespeare's source has not been established, but he presumably intended it to pass as a typically Jewish name. It may be from a biblical name that appeared, in the translations available in Shakespeare's day, as Jesca (Genesis 11:29; Iscah in the Authorized Version). This occurs in a somewhat obscure genealogical passage; Iscah appears to have been Abraham's niece. Notable bearers of the name include the British actress Jessica Tandy ( 1909–94 ), the British writer Jessica Mitford ( 1917–96 ), and the American actress Jessica Lange ( b. 1949 ). The name has been extremely popular since the 1990s.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

en.lifeSummary: When Jesse R. Smith Sr. was born on 31 January 1703, in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Thomas Smith Sr., was 55 and his mother, Mary Mayo, was 42. He married Sarah Higgins on 4 July 1724, in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 14 June 1782, in Carmel, Putnam, New York, United States, at the age of 79, and was buried in Dutchess, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jesse R. Smith Sr. est né le 31 janvier 1703, à Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas Smith Sr., avait 55 ans et sa mère, Mary Mayo, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Higgins le 4 juillet 1724, à Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 14 juin 1782, à Carmel, Putnam, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Dutchess, New York, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jesse R. Smith Sr. am 31. Januar 1703 in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Smith Sr. 55 und seine Mutter Mary Mayo 42. Er hat am 4. Juli 1724 in Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten Sarah Higgins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Juni 1782 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Carmel, Putnam, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Dutchess, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jesse R. Smith Sr. è nato il il 31 gennaio 1703 nel Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Thomas Smith Sr., aveva 55 anni e il suo madre, Mary Mayo, aveva 42 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Higgins il 4 luglio 1724, nel Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 14 giugno 1782, nel Carmel, Putnam, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolto a Dutchess, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Jesse R. Smith Sr.が1703年1月31日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルエアスサムで生まれた時，父Thomas Smith Sr.は55歳，母Mary Mayoは42歳であった。1724年7月4日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州バーンスタブルエアスサムでSarah Higginsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1782年6月14日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州プウトナムカーメルで79歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州ダトチェスにて埋葬された。

ko.Putnam, Charlotte, New York, British Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 찰로트 풋남
ko.Eastham, Plymouth Colony, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 이스섬
ko.Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬
ko.Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬
ko.lifeSummary: Jesse R. Smith Sr.(이)가 1703년 1월 31일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 이스섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Smith Sr.(은)는 55세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Mayo(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1724년 7월 4일 미국 매사추세츠 주 바른스타블일 이스섬에서 Sarah Higgins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1782년 6월 14일 미국 뉴욕 주 풋남 카멀에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 드트체스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jesse R. Smith Sr. nasceu em 31 de janeiro de 1703, em Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas Smith Sr., tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Mary Mayo, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Higgins em 4 de julho de 1724, em Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de junho de 1782, em Carmel, Putnam, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 79 anos, e foi sepultado em Dutchess, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Eastham, Plymouth Colony, British Coloni: эастам, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика

ru.Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay C: эастам, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jesse R. Smith Sr. родился 31 января 1703 г. в местности эастам, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Thomas Smith Sr. было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Mayo, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Higgins 4 июля 1724 г. в местности эастам, барнстабл, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 14 июня 1782 г. в местности кармел, путнам, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности дучесс, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jesse R. Smith Sr. nació el 31 de enero de 1703, en Eastham, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Smith Sr., tenía 55 años y su mamá, Mary Mayo, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Sarah Higgins el 4 de julio de 1724, en Eastham, Barnstable, Masachusets, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 14 de junio de 1782, en Carmel, Putnam, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultado en Dutchess, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
